mission.title: consul: UI rendering issues with Consul v1.15.0
mission.description: UI rendering issues with Consul v1.15.0. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current consul deployment
steps.description:
Verify your consul version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
helm list -n consul 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working consul installation.

steps.title: Review consul configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant consul configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul
```
After upgrading to Consul `v1.15.0`, we started to see rendering issues in UI. For example, the `Services` page doesn't render all list items when you navigate to the page.

steps.title: Apply the fix for UI rendering issues with Consul v1.15.0
steps.description:
Fixes the issue that was [reported here](https://github.com/hashicorp/consul/issues/16429).

Search filters were throwing errors and showing up empty. This also resulted in lists not showing full results until being interacted with. Weirdly enough I haven't been able to reproduce this against mock data despite the helper still being called on strings.

**Before:**
Running locally I tried logging the properties being passed into the "search across" filter and then logging them with the

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade consul to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade consul:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade consul consul/consul --namespace consul
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul
helm list -n consul
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "UI rendering issues with Consul v1.15.0" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Fixes the issue that was [reported here](https://github.com/hashicorp/consul/issues/16429).

Search filters were throwing errors and showing up empty. This also resulted in lists not showing full results until being interacted with. Weirdly enough I haven't been able to reproduce this against mock data despite the helper still being called on strings.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with consul installed or the issue environment reproducible.
